definition: Premium/Discount Arrays — the collective term for all institutional entry/exit zones. PD arrays are the building blocks of the ICT framework: every entry zone is a PD array, and understanding which arrays are premium vs discount determines whether to buy or sell at that zone.
feeds_into: optimal_trade_entry (OTE is applied within PD arrays)
entry_models (PD arrays ARE the entry zones — grade depends on which arrays are present)

premium_arrays.definition: Zones in the PREMIUM half of the dealing range (above 50%) — these are SELLING zones

arrays.name: Bearish Order Block
arrays.description: Last up-candle before a down-move in premium zone

arrays.name: Bearish FVG
arrays.description: Downside gap in premium — price returns to fill then drops

arrays.name: Bearish Breaker Block
arrays.description: Former bullish OB violated — now resistance

arrays.name: Bearish IFVG
arrays.description: Former bullish FVG violated and inverted

arrays.name: Liquidity Void (sell side)
arrays.description: Empty space from a sharp drop — price returns to fill

arrays.name: Old highs with BSL
arrays.description: Buy-side liquidity above old highs — sweep target

arrays.name: Rejection Block
arrays.description: Long upper wick in premium — selling pressure

premium_arrays.rule: Only SELL at premium arrays. Buying in premium = buying expensive = low probability.

discount_arrays.definition: Zones in the DISCOUNT half of the dealing range (below 50%) — these are BUYING zones

arrays.name: Bullish Order Block
arrays.description: Last down-candle before an up-move in discount zone

arrays.name: Bullish FVG
arrays.description: Upside gap in discount — price returns to fill then rises

arrays.name: Bullish Breaker Block
arrays.description: Former bearish OB violated — now support

arrays.name: Bullish IFVG
arrays.description: Former bearish FVG violated and inverted

arrays.name: Liquidity Void (buy side)
arrays.description: Empty space from a sharp rally — price returns to fill

arrays.name: Old lows with SSL
arrays.description: Sell-side liquidity below old lows — sweep target

arrays.name: Rejection Block
arrays.description: Long lower wick in discount — buying pressure

discount_arrays.rule: Only BUY at discount arrays. Selling in discount = selling cheap = low probability.

stacking_rules.definition: When multiple PD arrays overlap at the same price level — creates a 'stack'
stacking_rules.stacking_principle: More arrays at the same price = more institutional orders = higher probability of reaction

strongest_to_weakest: Propulsion Block (extreme displacement OB — near 90% first-touch reaction)
Breaker Block + FVG stack (Unicorn Model — reversal with double confirmation)
Unmitigated HTF OB + FVG (first touch of stacked PD arrays)
Single FVG (moderate, but needs OB or CE for precision)
Rejection Block (weakest — just a wick, no structural basis)
Liquidity Void (fill target, not entry zone)

ce_within_pd_arrays.rule: Always calculate the CE (50% midpoint) of every PD array
ce_within_pd_arrays.why: CE is where institutional order density is highest within the zone
ce_within_pd_arrays.entry_preference: Enter at CE of the strongest PD array in the stack, not at the zone edge
ce_within_pd_arrays.sl_placement: SL beyond the FAR edge of the array (not CE)

bridge_integration.array_detection: Pipeline detects OBs, FVGs, breakers independently — PD arrays is the umbrella classification
bridge_integration.stacking_detection: Check for price overlap between detected OBs and FVGs — flag stacks for higher scoring
bridge_integration.premium_discount_gate: Only trade premium arrays for SELL setups, discount arrays for BUY setups
bridge_integration.score_mapping: Number and quality of PD arrays at entry directly maps to ob_score + fvg_score in ICT pipeline
